title: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper.
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook spaghetti until al dente.
While pasta cooks, fry guanciale in a large pan over medium heat until crispy.
Whisk together eggs, grated Pecorino Romano, and plenty of black pepper in a bowl.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Remove pan from heat, add drained pasta to guanciale and toss well.
Add egg mixture and a splash of pasta water, tossing quickly to create a creamy sauce.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and black pepper.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender chicken in a rich, creamy tomato-based curry sauce with aromatic spices.
steps: Marinate chicken in yoghurt, half the spices, salt, garlic and ginger for at least 1 hour.
Grill or pan-fry chicken until charred and cooked through. Set aside.
Fry onions in oil until golden brown, about 12 minutes.
Add remaining garlic, ginger, and spices. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add tinned tomatoes and simmer for 15 minutes until sauce thickens.
Blend sauce until smooth, return to pan.
Add chicken and cream, simmer for 10 minutes.
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with basmati rice or naan.

title: Avocado Toast with Poached Eggs
description: Creamy smashed avocado on sourdough toast topped with perfectly poached eggs.
steps: Toast sourdough slices until golden.
Halve avocados, remove stone, and scoop flesh into a bowl.
Mash with lemon juice, salt, and pepper to your preferred texture.
Bring a pan of water with white wine vinegar to a gentle simmer.
Crack eggs into small cups and gently lower into simmering water.
Poach for 3 minutes for a runny yolk.
Spread avocado on toast, top with poached egg and chilli flakes.

title: Beef Tacos
description: Crispy corn tortillas filled with seasoned ground beef, fresh salsa, and sour cream.
steps: Dice onion and mince garlic, fry in oil until softened.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking up lumps.
Add cumin, paprika, chilli powder and salt. Cook for 2 minutes.
Halve cherry tomatoes for a quick salsa with lime juice and salt.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side.
Fill tortillas with beef mixture.
Top with cheese, sour cream, salsa and fresh coriander.

title: Mushroom Risotto
description: Velvety Arborio rice with mixed mushrooms, white wine, and Parmesan cheese.
steps: Keep stock warm in a separate pan over low heat.
Fry sliced mushrooms in butter until golden. Set aside.
Soften diced onion in olive oil for 8 minutes.
Add garlic and thyme, cook 1 minute.
Add Arborio rice and stir to coat in oil for 2 minutes.
Pour in white wine and stir until absorbed.
Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly, allowing each addition to absorb before adding next.
After 20 minutes, stir in mushrooms, Parmesan, and a knob of butter.
Season, rest for 2 minutes, and serve immediately.

title: Classic Burger
description: Juicy beef patty with melted cheese, crispy lettuce, and a brioche bun.
steps: Divide mince into 4 equal balls, season generously with salt and pepper.
Press each ball into a patty slightly wider than the bun.
Heat a cast iron pan or grill over high heat until very hot.
Cook patties 3–4 minutes per side for medium.
Place cheese on patty in the final minute and cover to melt.
Toast buns cut-side down in the pan for 1 minute.
Assemble with sauce, lettuce, tomato, onion, patty, and top bun.

title: Greek Salad
description: Fresh cucumber, tomatoes, olives, and feta with a simple olive oil dressing.
steps: Chop cucumber into chunky half-moons.
Cut tomatoes into wedges.
Thinly slice red onion.
Combine cucumber, tomatoes, onion, and olives in a large bowl.
Lay feta block on top and drizzle generously with olive oil.
Sprinkle with dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Serve with crusty bread to mop up the juices.

title: Pad Thai
description: Stir-fried rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, egg, and classic tamarind sauce.
steps: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 30 minutes, drain.
Mix tamarind paste, fish sauce, and palm sugar for the sauce.
Heat wok over high heat until smoking.
Fry tofu until golden, push to one side.
Add prawns and cook until pink.
Crack in eggs and scramble briefly.
Add noodles and sauce, toss everything together over high heat.
Add bean sprouts and spring onions, toss for 1 minute.
Serve with peanuts, lime wedge, and chilli flakes.

title: Banana Bread
description: Moist, fluffy loaf packed with ripe bananas and a hint of vanilla.
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C and grease a 9x5 inch loaf tin.
Mash bananas thoroughly in a large bowl.
Stir in melted butter.
Mix in sugar, beaten eggs, and vanilla extract.
Sprinkle in baking soda and salt, stir to combine.
Fold in flour until just combined — do not over mix.
Pour into loaf tin and bake for 55–65 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool in tin for 10 minutes before turning out.

title: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a one-pan wonder.
steps: Soften diced onion and pepper in olive oil for 10 minutes.
Add garlic and spices, stir for 1 minute.
Add tinned tomatoes, season and simmer for 10 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook over low heat for 6–8 minutes until whites are set.
Scatter with fresh parsley and serve with crusty bread.

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
description: Decadent chocolate cakes with a gooey, molten centre — ready in under 30 minutes.
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C. Butter and dust 4 ramekins with cocoa powder.
Melt chocolate and butter together in a bowl over simmering water.
Whisk eggs, yolks, and sugar until pale and thick.
Fold chocolate mixture into egg mixture.
Sift in flour and fold gently until combined.
Divide between ramekins and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Bake for 10–12 minutes — edges set, centre still soft.
Run a knife around edge and turn out onto plates. Serve immediately.

title: Salmon Teriyaki Bowl
description: Glazed salmon fillets over steamed rice with edamame and pickled ginger.
steps: Cook jasmine rice according to packet instructions.
Mix soy sauce, mirin, and honey to make teriyaki glaze.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side down and cook for 4 minutes.
Flip, brush generously with teriyaki glaze, cook for 3 more minutes.
Brush again with glaze and cook for 1 final minute.
Serve salmon over rice with edamame, pickled ginger, spring onions and sesame seeds.
